entries.value: Carbon bakes keyboard operation into its components, improving the experience of blind users and others who operate via the keyboard.
Pagination is used for splitting up content or data into several pages, with a control for navigating to the next or previous page.
Generally, pagination is used if there are more than 25 items displayed in one view.
The default number displayed will vary depending on the context.
Clearly identify which page the user is on by displaying the current page number.
By providing context into how many pages there are in total (eg. 1 of 4 pages), you can help provide clarity around the data displayed.
Previous and next chevrons or links are the most useful way for the user to move forward or backward through pages of data.
Provide an inline select in which users can choose the page they wish to navigate to.
Pagination text should be set in sentence case with the first letter of each word capitalized.
The Pagination bar is most commonly used in data tables, and should span the entire width of the table it’s being paired with.
Pagination width varies based on content and layout
When the pagination is at either end of its range, one of the page navigation buttons becomes invalid. it's no longer navigable or operable, like any other disabled control.
“Too many results” can be influenced by system load times, amount of data in each entry or screen space
When the number of pages exceeds the maximum display limit, an ellipsis is used to truncate the remaining pages.
Double truncation is used when the current page is separated by more than five pages from both the first and last page.
Commonly used for things like table listings, search results, and directories.
Sits at the bottom of the page and allows the user to easily move between each page.
Each page in the pagination control is a button link that allows the them to quickly navigate through the results.
For smaller screens, it's best to show a maximum of seven pages (including the ellipsis).
For larger screens, show a maximum of 14 pages (including the ellipsis).
Every page should have a URL.
Paginate content that would be easier to browse when it is segmented.
Place Pagination at the end of the page content.
If the content is only long enough for a single page, don't render Pagination.
Pagination allows you to divide large amounts of content into smaller chunks across multiple pages.
Pagination splits content or data into several pages, with a control for navigating to the next or previous page
Pagination is a horizontal set of links to navigate paginated content.
Use an inline select within the pagination bar so the user can change the amount of data displayed per page.
Use pagination when there are too many results to show on the one page, so the user isn't overwhelmed by too much information.
Do center align pagination beneath search results.
Do place pagination at the bottom of search results.
Do show the first, previous, next, and last page links when possible. If not, use the degraded designs.
Don't show too many or too few items per page. too many items per page could be overwhelming, too few items per page could be frustrating to browse.
Do not use pagination superfluously, and aim to use it to improve usability or performance.
Do not use it to display linear journeys, for example, in a form progression. Instead, use the progress bar or button components to navigate forward and backward.
Don't right or left align pagination.
Don't place pagination above search results.
Don't show all available pages.
